title: Who May File
content: An aggrieved person may file a complaint in writing and under oath for violation of laws, rules and regulations or other issuances relating to overseas employment. An aggrieved person is one who is prejudiced by the commission of a violation as enumerated in the immediately preceding rules. The Administration may, on its own initiative, conduct proceedings based on reports of violation of these Rules and other issuances on overseas employment, subject to preliminary evaluation. The report should state clearly the facts constituting the offense, attaching all available evidence relative thereto. The report shall be investigated by the Adjudication Office or the POEA regional center/extension units.
